prompt: Find the PID of a running TextEdit process via 'pgrep TextEdit', kill it with kill -TERM, verify it's gone by re-running pgrep. Write 'killed PID <N>' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/296-kill.txt. Fast path: cerebellum 'terminal run "mkdir -p ~/Desktop/kinbench && PID=$(pgrep -x TextEdit | head -n1) && kill -TERM $PID 2>/dev/null && echo killed PID $PID > ~/Desktop/kinbench/296-kill.txt"'.
